item.title: How to keep AC costs low this summer
item.description: Small changes to your cooling habits and HVAC system can lead to meaningful savings on your electric bill.

item.title: U.K. far-right leader Nigel Farage resigns as lawmaker in risky gamble
item.description: Populist British lawmaker Nigel Farage has resigned amid a financial scandal, but says he'll run in a snap election to reclaim his seat.

item.title: Gotham FC's governor says club's move to NYC is "the next chapter"
item.description: Gotham FC's announced Tuesday the women's soccer team is moving home games​ from New Jersey to the Etihad Park stadium in Queens, New York.

item.title: Tankers attacked as Iran says it could abandon talks over Trump's threats
item.description: As many as 3 tankers come under attack in the Strait of Hormuz as the U.S. and Iran continue exchanging threats with peace talks on hold.

item.title: Trump says U.S. will lift sanctions on Turkey
item.description: President Trump announced at the NATO summit in Turkey that the U.S. is set to lift sanctions on the country after meeting with President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan, calling Turkey "much more loyal than other countries." The sanctions were previously imposed after the purchase of a Russian missile system. Olivia Gazis reports.

item.title: Video shows "well fed" great white shark eating whale off Rhode Island
item.description: A great white shark was captured feeding on the carcass of a humpback whale near Rhode Island, in rare video footage.

item.title: Trump meeting with NATO leaders including Erdoğan, Zelenskyy
item.description: President Trump is meeting with various world leaders at the NATO summit in Turkey, including Turkish President Recep Erdoğan and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy. CBS News' Ed O'Keefe and former CIA officer Elliot Ackerman comment on the political agendas for Mr. Trump's meetings.

item.title: NATO must unite air defense capacities for Ukraine to defeat Russia, Ukrainian politician says
item.description: President Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy will meet at a NATO summit in Turkey. Ukrainian Parliament member Halyna Yanchenko looks ahead to the discussion and stresses the breadth of support Ukraine is hoping for.

item.title: Can't afford more than the minimum credit card payment? Here are your options.
item.description: Making the minimum payments keeps your card account current, but it doesn't keep you out of financial trouble.

item.title: Gotham FC will leave New Jersey, relocate to Queens' Etihad Park in 2028
item.description: National Women's Soccer League champs Gotham FC will move their home games from New Jersey to Queens, New York, by 2028. Gotham FC's new home will be the Etihad Park soccer stadium.

item.title: USMNT head coach reacts after Belgium crushes FIFA World Cup bid
item.description: The U.S. Men's National Team is out of the FIFA World Cup tournament after a 4-1 loss to Belgium. Apple TV commentator Steve Cangialosi reacts to the elimination.

item.title: Walmart cuts prices on thousands of items, including beef and soda
item.description: President Trump praised Walmart and said the lower prices are the result of "my Administration's request to celebrate our great Country's 250th birthday."

item.title: French President Macron safe after bomb blasts near hotel in Damascus
item.description: France's government says President Emmanuel Macron is safe and will continue his visit to Syria after two bombs exploded outside his hotel in Damascus.

item.title: Nara Organics infant formula linked to another case of botulism
item.description: The FDA is urging parents and caregivers to immediately stop using a Nara Organics-brand formula after several infants contracted botulism.

item.title: A look at Prince Harry's various legal battles against the U.K. media
item.description: Prince Harry has lost his lawsuit against the publisher of the Daily Mail newspaper, but his other legal battles against Britain's tabloids were more successful.

item.title: Ex-college hoops star accused of posing as other people in $2.2M scam
item.description: Kerr Kriisa posed as other people and falsely claimed he and his family urgently needed money to deal with an emergency, the indictment says.

item.title: Can surviving spouses be sued for medical debt?
item.description: Wondering who's responsible for the remaining medical bills after a spouse dies? The answer may surprise you.

item.title: Trump meets with Turkish President Erdogan ahead of NATO summit
item.description: President Trump and Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan met on Tuesday in Ankara ahead of a NATO summit. CBS News' Ed O'Keefe and former CIA officer and Marine Corps veteran Elliot Ackerman join with more.

item.title: Gotham FC announces it's relocating from New Jersey to Queens, New York
item.description: Gotham FC, the reigning National Women's Soccer League champions, announced they will relocate their home games from New Jersey to Etihad Park in Queens, New York, beginning in 2028. Gotham FC Governor Carolyn Tisch Blodgett talks to "CBS Mornings" about what this move means for the team and the impact of having a professional women's soccer team playing in New York.

item.title: Prince Harry loses long legal battle against U.K. tabloid publisher
item.description: Prince Harry has lost his case against the publisher of the Daily Mail and Mail on Sunday tabloids, with the top U.K. court dismissing claims of illegal information gathering.

item.title: Explosions rock Damascus during Macron visit in Syria
item.description: Explosions in Damascus injured over a dozen people, according to Syrian state TV, during French leader Emmanuel Macron's visit to Syria to meet with President Ahmed al-Sharaa. CBS News' Chris Livesay has more.

item.title: Video shows moment roof collapses as flash flooding slams East Coast
item.description: Parts of the East Coast are cleaning up from a series of storms that brought flash flooding and widespread damage. Meg Oliver reports.

item.title: Trump lauds Erdogan, announces lifting sanctions as NATO summit begins
item.description: Amid simmering tensions between the U.S. and some NATO allies over Iran and Greenland, President Trump will continue to press other countries to boost defense spending.

item.title: Democrats call for Graham Platner to abandon Senate race after sexual assault accusation
item.description: Democratic leadership is pushing Maine's Graham Platner to remove himself from the Senate race after a former partner accused Platner of sexual assault. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns has the latest details.

item.title: Common talks "Silo" series and what drew him to the role
item.description: Common speaks to "CBS Mornings" about starring in the series "Silo," which is back for a third season. The Oscar, Emmy and Grammy Award winner reveals what drew him to his character. He also discusses recent performances, including at the BET Awards and the opening of the Obama Presidential Center.

item.title: Belgium dominates U.S. in World Cup match after red card controversy
item.description: Belgium eliminated the U.S. from the World Cup on Monday with a dominating 4-1 victory. The U.S. loss followed days of controversy regarding U.S. star Folarin Balogun's red card suspension and FIFA's decision to lift the one-game ban. Nicole Valdes reports.

item.title: Oprah's Book Club pick Sophie Chen Keller reads "Little Wonder" excerpt
item.description: Oprah Winfrey selected "Little Wonder" by acclaimed author Sophie Chen Keller as her latest book club pick. Keller reads an excerpt from her novel about an extraordinary journey of hope and love.

item.title: French right-wing leader Marine Le Pen cleared to run in 2027 election
item.description: Le Pen is now eligible to run in the forthcoming election, but would have to do so wearing an electronic tag, something she has previously ruled out.

item.title: Woman suspected in bomb attack on Ukrainian magnate found dead in Kyiv
item.description: A woman suspected of planting a bomb that targeted a wealthy Ukrainian businessman in Monaco has been found shot dead in Kyiv.

item.title: Weather service faces hurricane season with less experienced staff, missing data
item.description: The National Weather Service is hiring hundreds of entry-level employees after losing about 15% of its staff to federal cuts last year.
